title: HIGH: CORS allows any origin with full access
description: In src/server.rs lines 14-18, the CORS layer is configured with allow_origin(Any), allow_methods(Any), allow_headers(Any), expose_headers(Any). This is standard for Mastodon-compatible APIs (Mastodon itself uses Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) since browser clients like Elk and Pinafore run on different origins and use Bearer tokens. With wildcard origin, browsers do not send credentials (cookies), so the risk is limited to scenarios where a malicious site already possesses a valid Bearer token. However, expose_headers(Any) is more permissive than needed -- consider restricting to the specific headers clients need (Link, X-RateLimit-*, etc.).
close_reason: By design: Mastodon Client API requires permissive CORS for browser-based clients (Elk, Phanpy, Pinafore)
